display.purpose: Brand wordmarks, scorecard landmarks, and major social titles only.

title.purpose: Screen section heroes, modal titles, and major summaries.

heading.purpose: Card titles, row names, and compact section headings.

body.purpose: Operational copy, metadata, helper text, and descriptions.

label.purpose: Chips, counters, badges, and compact controls.

shadows.name: card
shadows.purpose: Light-mode cards and list rows that need separation from Warm Canvas.

shadows.name: elevated
shadows.purpose: Modal-like panels and stronger surface separation.

shadows.name: accent
shadows.value: Clubhouse Green shadowOpacity 0.2, offset 0 2, radius 8, elevation 3
shadows.purpose: Primary action buttons in light mode.

motion.name: state-feedback
motion.purpose: Short state transitions and touch feedback.

motion.name: splash-breathe
motion.purpose: Loading splash golf ball breathing motion.

motion.name: splash-ring
motion.purpose: Loading splash ring pulse, ceremonial only.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main action button for starting, saving, sharing, joining, and confirming flows.

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Back, cancel, and secondary action button with a quiet border vocabulary.

components.name: Text Field
components.description: Default text input and search field shape used across setup, auth, and libraries.

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Compact pill for gallery filters, round selectors, and segmented choices.

components.name: Section Card
components.description: Standard compact content shell for stats, lists, setup blocks, and scoring panels.

components.name: Bottom Sheet
components.description: Focused choice and sharing surface with rounded top corners and a modest handle.
components.html:
Invite players

Share this link with friends joining the round.
components.css: .ds-sheet{background:#ffffff;color:#1a1a1a;border:1px solid #ece8e1;border-radius:22px 22px 0 0;padding:22px 22px 28px;font-family:PlusJakartaSans,system-ui,sans-serif;max-width:420px}.ds-sheet-handle{width:42px;height:4px;border-radius:2px;background:#ece8e1;margin:0 auto 18px}.ds-sheet h2{margin:0;color:#1a1a1a;font-family:PlayfairDisplay-Bold,PlayfairDisplay,Georgia,serif;font-size:26px;font-weight:700;line-height:32px;letter-spacing:-.3px;text-align:center}.ds-sheet p{margin:8px 0 18px;color:#6b7280;font-size:13px;font-weight:500;line-height:19px;text-align:center}.ds-sheet .ds-btn-primary{display:inline-flex;width:100%;align-items:center;justify-content:center;gap:8px;background:#006747;color:#ffffff;border:0;border-radius:14px;padding:14px 16px;font-family:PlusJakartaSans-ExtraBold,PlusJakartaSans,system-ui,sans-serif;font-size:14px;font-weight:800;line-height:20px;min-height:48px;cursor:pointer;transition:background-color .18s ease,transform .18s ease}.ds-sheet .ds-btn-primary:hover{background:#005538}.ds-sheet .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible{outline:3px solid rgba(0,103,71,.28);outline-offset:2px}

narrative.overview:
Golf Partner should feel like a familiar clubhouse table after a round: scores are present, names matter, and the interface supports friendly rivalry without turning the group into tournament operators. The system is a restrained product UI with one clear green accent, warm neutral surfaces, concise cards, and direct mobile controls.

The app already carries two visual voices. Plus Jakarta Sans does the operational work for labels, buttons, scores, and lists. Playfair Display is reserved for brand moments, modal titles, and scorecard landmarks where the app can feel more social and memorable. Keep that split. Do not let the serif become routine UI chrome.
keyCharacteristics: People and recency lead before raw scoring tables.
Green marks action, selection, current state, and official golf identity.
Cards are compact containers, not decorative page sections.
Mobile outdoor use wins over visual flourish: high contrast, clear tap targets, and stable layouts.
Social surfaces may feel warm; scoring surfaces must stay fast and unambiguous.

rules.name: The Green Means Action Rule
rules.body: Green is for primary actions, selected filters, live state, pair A, and excellent scoring. If everything is green, nothing is actionable.

rules.name: The Gold Is Ceremony Rule
rules.body: Gold belongs to splash, rank, and special score moments. It is prohibited for normal buttons, tabs, or helper text.

rules.name: The Neutral Must Read Rule
rules.body: Muted text must stay legible outdoors. If a label competes with sunlight or a tinted background, move it from Olive Muted to Slate or Ink.

rules.name: The Sans Does The Work Rule
rules.body: Buttons, tabs, scoring cells, inputs, tables, and state labels always use Plus Jakarta Sans.

rules.name: The Serif Earns The Moment Rule
rules.body: Playfair Display is allowed for brand, modal, and scorecard moments. It is forbidden inside dense controls, small labels, and data rows.

rules.name: The Touch Only Rule
rules.body: Elevation exists to separate controls, sheets, tabs, and tappable cards. It is not background decoration.

rules.name: The Dark Mode Layer Rule
rules.body: In dark mode, use tonal layers and borders first. Heavy black shadows on already-dark surfaces are prohibited.

dos: Do lead with people, recency, and active game state before dense scoring data.
Do keep scoring controls compact, predictable, and readable on mobile outdoors.
Do use Clubhouse Green for primary actions, selected state, and live state only.
Do reserve Playfair Display for brand and scorecard moments; use Plus Jakarta Sans for everything operational.
Do keep cards at 14 to 16px radius unless they are true pills, balls, or bottom sheets.
Do pair color with text, icon, rank, or layout for score quality and error states.
Do keep web and Android surfaces visually aligned from the same token vocabulary.
donts: Don't make the app feel like enterprise sports admin dashboards.
Don't create sterile spreadsheet-like leaderboards that hide who played and what happened.
Don't make a generic social feed that hides the golf context.
Don't add decorative golf nostalgia that slows down scoring workflows.
Don't use gold for everyday CTAs, tabs, search fields, or filters.
Don't put Playfair Display inside buttons, chips, dense rows, or score inputs.
Don't rely on color alone for rankings, selected filters, score quality, or errors.
Don't use side-stripe card borders, gradient text, glassmorphism, or repeated identical marketing card grids.
